text.title: Load Balancer Types and Algorithm Selection Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules including a `skip-this-methodology` rule. Each pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Select L7 for HTTP/HTTPS workloads; L4 only for non-HTTP traffic (DB, mail, raw TCP/UDP, game servers).
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Default Round Robin for stateless equal-capacity backends; switch to Least Connections when request duration variance exceeds 10×.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Consistent Hashing MUST be confined to cache-tier load balancers (Varnish, CDN origin, Redis cluster). Forbidden for general HTTP traffic.

rationale.source: Cache locality vs traffic skew analysis

rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: IP-hash MUST NOT be used as session-affinity for general web traffic — NAT and mobile networks break it.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Weighted algorithms MUST tie weight values to measured backend capacity, not arbitrary integers.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Skip when (a) single instance OR (b) cloud LB default already accepted with no ADR requirement.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.
